summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a high‑energy show highlighted by an epic Hell in a Bucket and strong High Time performance
review: The recording is generally clear with a solid soundboard mix, though some listeners note the overall level is a bit low. Musically the concert shines on “Hell in a Bucket,” “High Time,” and Bob Weir’s wild vocal delivery, delivering an exciting first set and a lively second set despite occasional weariness in the later numbers.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and note good sound quality, but a few point out vocal issues and low volume, leading to an overall solid but not flawless rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear and balanced overall; some listeners mention the mix sounds a bit low in level but no major distortion or drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set rides a friendly, upbeat vibe with “Touch,” “Minglewood” and the highlight “Hell in a Bucket.” The second set keeps momentum with strong renditions of “High Time,” “Fire,” and a spirited close, though a few later cuts feel slightly weary.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice sounded strained (“frog in his throat”) on some numbers, but his guitar work remained solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass provides a plucky, supportive finish; generally praised as steady and reliable.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir delivers high‑energy vocals and “screaming” moments, especially on “Hell in a Bucket,” earning rave remarks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard tones receive positive notes for depth and coloring, highlighted in several songs.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum mix is described as distant but adequate; no major complaints.
